text:
3. Inject uses only real nouns as a first-mention primer.
   If the current prompt mentions a real project noun for the first time in this session, `pc` prepends a small block with its definition and prompt-relevant facts. It is explicitly a separate primer, not blended into normal retrieval. See [src/nouns.rs](/Users/pablofernandez/src/proactive-context/src/nouns.rs:831), [src/nouns.rs](/Users/pablofernandez/src/proactive-context/src/nouns.rs:937), and [src/nouns.rs](/Users/pablofernandez/src/proactive-context/src/nouns.rs:1473). [...]    Run 14 confirmed that the facts-level noun primer improved noun grounding from 28.6% to 57.1% with zero drift, and recommended shipping `facts` as default. See [run14-noun-primer-results.md](/Users/pablofernandez/src/proactive-context/docs/product-spec/run14-noun-primer-results.md:27) and [run14-noun-primer-results.md](/Users/pablofernandez/src/proactive-context/docs/product-spec/run14-noun-primer-results.md:100).
